title: The Necklace of Princess Fiorimonde
description: The Necklace of Princess Fiorimonde — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "The Necklace of Princess Fiorimonde" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: "Contents"
memes.description: "Table of contents for The Necklace of Princess Fiorimonde"

memes.label: Aldebert
memes.description: The second of King Roland's three nephews to be crowned, who eagerly follows all advice but pleases no one, and abandons the throne to become a travelling tinker.

memes.label: Alderete
memes.description: The youngest of King Roland's three nephews, whose overbearing micromanagement as king — including banning pea soup — drives his subjects to revolt and himself to become a chimney sweep.

memes.label: Aldovrand
memes.description: The eldest of King Roland's three nephews, who is crowned king first but abandons the throne after one day, preferring the simple life of a goose-herd.

memes.label: Arasmon
memes.description: A wandering musician whose beautiful lute-playing enchants all who hear it. He searches the world for his lost wife Chrysea for many long years, never losing hope.

memes.label: Beauty and Deception
memes.description: "A central theme in Mary De Morgan's tales: physical beauty concealing inner wickedness, challenging the fairy-tale convention that beauty equals virtue."

memes.label: characters-index
memes.description: Complete index of all characters appearing in Mary De Morgan's "The Necklace of Princess Fiorimonde and Other Stories," dynamically generated from wiki entries.

memes.label: Chrysea
memes.description: A wandering musician and singer, wife of Arasmon, whose beautiful voice could move listeners to tears and who bravely broke the spell over an enchanted village.

memes.label: "Colophon"

memes.label: Death
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, death appears as a narrative presence rather than a horror — the natural terminus of sacrifice, the proof of love, and the quiet transformation that marks the end of a journey."

memes.label: Death and Mortality
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, death is rarely final — it hovers as a threat, a transformation, or a fate evaded through enchantment, but true death is deferred by the fairy-tale logic that governs her world."

memes.label: Despair
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, despair is the dark night of the hero's trial — a necessary station on the path to triumph, not a final destination."

memes.label: Despair and Endurance
memes.description: "De Morgan's 1880 tales trace the arc from hope to despair and back — characters who endure through the darkest passages of despair emerge transformed, while those who surrender to it are consumed."

memes.label: events-index
memes.description: Index of significant narrative events, plot turning points, and key moments across Mary De Morgan's seven fairy tales, dynamically generated from wiki entries.

memes.label: Evil
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, evil is not an abstract force but a visible, tangible corruption — manifesting as yellow eyes, wizened faces, and sorcery that ultimately consumes its wielder."

memes.label: Evil as Deception
memes.description: "De Morgan's 1880 collection portrays evil not as monstrous otherness but as beautiful deception — the witch's yellow face hides behind lovely masks, and the most dangerous villains are those who smile."

memes.label: Fate
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, fate is the narrative logic that ensures moral consequences — neither random fortune nor divine decree, but the inevitable outcome of choices made in the fairy-tale world."

memes.label: Fate and Transformation
memes.description: "Transformation as destiny in De Morgan's tales — characters are changed into beads, harps, and beasts, but their fates are never arbitrary: each metamorphosis reflects moral choice and inner nature."

memes.label: Fear
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, fear is the atmosphere of tyranny, the test of courage, and the barrier that music and love must dissolve."

memes.label: Fear and Power
memes.description: "Fear as an instrument of power and social control across De Morgan's tales — from Fiorimonde's reign of terror to the spellbound village's collective dread and the baker's persecution."

memes.label: "Front Matter"
memes.description: "# The Necklace of Princess Fiorimonde **Author:** Mary De Morgan # The Necklace of Princess Fiorimonde By **Mary De Morgan**."

memes.label: Gervaise
memes.description: The loyal friend of Prince Florestan who defeats the wicked Princess Fiorimonde by tricking her into grasping her own enchanted necklace, freeing all her victims.

memes.label: Greed and Beauty
memes.description: The dark counter-theme across Mary De Morgan's tales — beauty cultivated through selfishness, greed that consumes its possessor, and pride that invites fairy vengeance.

memes.label: Happiness
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, happiness is the natural state from which characters fall and to which they must return — never a permanent possession but a gift restored after trial."

memes.label: Happiness and Contentment
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, happiness is distinguished from joy as a quieter, steadier state — the contentment of living in accordance with one's nature rather than the ecstasy of liberation."

memes.label: Hope
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, hope is repeatedly deferred, tested, and finally rewarded — a fragile but persistent force that sustains characters through long trials."

memes.label: Hope as Resilience
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, hope functions not as naive optimism but as a form of moral resilience — characters who sustain hope endure through suffering, while those who abandon it court despair."

memes.label: "Imprint"
memes.description: "## Imprint ![The Standard Ebooks logo.](../images/logo.png) This ebook is the product of many hours of hard work by volunteers for"

memes.label: Joy
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, joy functions as a transformative threshold — the sudden emotional release that marks the moment when enchantment breaks, understanding dawns, or the lost is found."

memes.label: Joy as Emotion
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, joy is not a permanent state but a luminous moment — a sudden emergence from suffering or enchantment that marks the threshold between oppression and liberation."

memes.label: Justice
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, justice emerges through satire — the futility of human institutions contrasted with the poetic justice of fairy-tale logic, where the wicked are ensnared by their own devices."

memes.label: Justice and Injustice
memes.description: "The satire of judicial authority and the pursuit of true justice in De Morgan's tales — from the absurd trial of the baker to Fiorimonde's poetic fate and the restoration of the wronged."

memes.label: King Adrian
memes.description: One of the suitors of Princess Fiorimonde who was transformed into a bead on her enchanted gold necklace, later freed when the charm was broken.

memes.label: King Lambert
memes.description: The antagonist of "The Heart of Princess Joan," an old and ugly foreign king who attempted to marry Princess Joan for political gain, only to be thwarted by the return of Prince Michael.

memes.label: King Pierrot
memes.description: The first suitor of Princess Fiorimonde, a handsome young king from across the sea who was transformed into the first bead on her enchanted necklace.

memes.label: Love and Loyalty
memes.description: A central theme across Mary De Morgan's fairy tales — the testing of love through separation, enchantment, and sacrifice, and the loyalty that endures through years of suffering.

memes.label: Magic and Witchcraft
memes.description: "A pervasive theme in De Morgan's collection: the use and consequences of magic, from Fiorimonde's necklace to the elves' curses and fairy interventions."

memes.label: magical-items-index
memes.description: Index of all magical items, enchanted objects, and significant artifacts from Mary De Morgan's fairy tales, dynamically generated from wiki entries.

memes.label: Marriage
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, marriage is the ritual threshold where identity is transformed — a moment of danger as often as it is a moment of reward."

memes.label: Marriage and Union
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, marriage is a destination reached only after trial — a union earned through suffering, never merely bestowed, and the narrative's ultimate marker of restoration and moral order."

memes.label: Mary De Morgan
memes.description: Victorian author of literary fairy tales, best known for "The Necklace of Princess Fiorimonde and Other Stories" (1880). Her dark, morally complex tales stand apart from the sentimental children's literature of her era.

memes.label: Music
memes.description: The motif of music as a transformative force capable of enchanting listeners, expressing the inexpressible, and bridging worlds in De Morgan's fairy tales.

memes.label: Music as Art
memes.description: "Instrumental music as a redemptive art form in De Morgan's tales — Arasmon's lute and harp embody music's power to preserve love, restore life, and transcend time."

memes.label: places-index
memes.description: Index of all significant locations, kingdoms, and settings across Mary De Morgan's seven fairy tales, dynamically generated from wiki entries.

memes.label: Prince Florestan
memes.description: A young prince who becomes the eleventh suitor of Princess Fiorimonde and is transformed into a bead on her necklace, later rescued by his loyal friend Gervaise.

memes.label: Prince Hildebrandt
memes.description: The second suitor of Princess Fiorimonde, a tall fair prince from a northern country who was lured into the garden at sunrise and transformed into the second bead on her enchanted necklace.

memes.label: Prince Michael
memes.description: The devoted prince who embarks on a seven-year quest to recover Princess Joan's stolen heart from a fairy, ultimately winning her love and rescuing her from a loveless marriage.

memes.label: Princess Fernanda
memes.description: The protagonist of "The Wise Princess," a princess who mastered all knowledge from a wizard but could not learn the secret of happiness until Death taught her through self-sacrifice.

memes.label: Princess Fiorimonde
memes.description: The beautiful but vain princess who uses a magical necklace to transform her suitors into beads, ultimately facing the consequences of her cruelty in Mary De Morgan's title story.

memes.label: Princess Joan
memes.description: The beautiful but enchanted princess whose heart was stolen at birth by a vengeful fairy, leaving her incapable of love or sorrow until Prince Michael's seven-year quest restores it.

memes.label: Punishment and Reward
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, punishment and reward are the narrative events that restore moral equilibrium — villains are unmade by their own magic, and heroes are granted union and recognition as the earned consequence of their choices."

memes.label: Singing
memes.description: The recurring motif of singing as a form of power, expression, and transformation across Mary De Morgan's fairy tales.

memes.label: Singing as Power
memes.description: "A recurring motif across De Morgan's tales: singing as a form of enchantment, liberation, and emotional expression — from Chrysea's spell-breaking song to Fiorimonde's seductive dawn serenade."

memes.label: The Acorn Necklace
memes.description: "The simple necklace of haws, acorns, and hips that Gervaise strings together as a decoy — the humble counterpoint to Fiorimonde's golden chain of destruction."

memes.label: The Baker's Oven
memes.description: The oven in the village bakery where a dark elf dwells and bakes enchanted bread that causes discontent in all who eat it, in the tale "The Bread of Discontent."

memes.label: The Beads (Enchanted Necklace)
memes.description: The magical necklace created by an old witch for Princess Fiorimonde. Each bead is a transformed prince or king who tried to marry her, trapped by the golden thread's sorcery.

memes.label: The Black Castle
memes.description: The heart-prison fortress in "The Heart of Princess Joan" — a gloomy black marble castle where stolen hearts are kept, guarded by an old gnome and his serpent.

memes.label: The Scarlet Blanket
memes.description: A small scarlet blanket from the pedlar's pack in "The Pedlar's Pack," serving as the catalyst for a chain of broken promises among four travelling companions — a pedlar, a donkey, a raven, and a hedge-sparrow.

memes.label: "The Bread of Discontent"
memes.description: The sixth story in Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, a satirical fable about an imp who bakes discontent into bread, causing an entire village to find fault with their lives.

memes.label: The Breaking of Enchantments
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, the breaking of an enchantment is the climactic event that reverses magical harm — a moment of liberation that restores the enchanted to their true form and signals the triumph of knowledge or love over dark magic."

memes.label: The Exchange of Gifts
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, the exchange of gifts is a narrative event that tests character — the right gift heals, the wrong gift entraps, and the true gift is given without expectation of return."

memes.label: The Farmhouse
memes.description: A large, prosperous farm in "The Three Clever Kings" where the ex-king Aldovrand finds contentment as a goose-boy after abandoning the throne.

memes.label: The Fateful Bargain
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, the fateful bargain is a transaction with hidden costs — characters strike deals that seem advantageous but carry consequences understood only after the agreement is sealed."

memes.label: The Fishing Village
memes.description: A small coastal village in "The Wanderings of Arasmon" where Arasmon encounters fisherwomen anxiously watching for their husbands lost in a sea mist.

memes.label: The Gold Necklace
memes.description: The magical necklace given to Princess Fiorimonde by the old witch; it transforms anyone who grasps it into a bead, trapping them until the string is cut.

memes.label: The Gold Thread
memes.description: The magical gold thread drawn from the witch's black bag — the core component of the enchanted necklace in Mary De Morgan's tale.

memes.label: The Golden Harp
memes.description: A magical golden harp created by the elves of the moor, formed from the transformed body of the singer Chrysea, whose voice lives on in its enchanted strings.

memes.label: The Golden Sword
memes.description: A gleaming golden sword set with precious stones, hung high on the wizard's wall as both weapon and key — the only means by which Prince Michael can enter the enchanted castle to recover the heart of Princess Joan.

memes.label: The Heart of Princess Joan
memes.description: The third tale in Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, telling the story of a princess whose heart was stolen by a fairy, and the prince who quests seven years to recover it.

memes.label: The Hero's Journey
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, the hero's journey is a departure into hardship that tests character through labour, deception, and endurance — a trial that transforms the hero before it transforms their circumstances."

memes.label: The Hero's Return
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, the hero's return is a narrative climax — the moment when the absent protagonist reappears, often in disguise, to reclaim their place and restore moral order."

memes.label: The Iron Manacles
memes.description: Heavy iron manacles and chains placed upon Prince Michael by the old wizard, binding his arms and legs to restrict his movement during seven years of labour on the serpent's island.

memes.label: The Magic Glass
memes.description: A small round piece of enchanted glass given to Prince Michael by the sorcerer, enabling him to see the true colours of stars and to pierce through magical illusions to reveal hidden realities.

memes.label: The Magic Peas
memes.description: "The enchanted seeds that Princess Fiorimonde feeds to a small brown bird, transforming it into a giant flying steed for her nightly journeys to the witch's mountain."

memes.label: The Magic Water
memes.description: "The few drops from a small black bottle that reverse the pea-induced transformation of Fiorimonde's bird — the quiet mechanism of return that sustains her secret double life."

memes.label: The Moorland
memes.description: A charred waste moor near the Spellbound Village where the wizard and his dark elves dwelled and renewed their curse each night, in "The Wanderings of Arasmon."

memes.label: "The Necklace of Princess Fiorimonde (Story)"
memes.description: The title story of Mary De Morgan's 1880 fairy tale collection, in which a beautiful but wicked princess transforms her suitors into beads on an enchanted necklace.

memes.label: The Old Witch
memes.description: The wicked and hideous sorceress who lives in a hut on a lonely mountain and teaches Princess Fiorimonde witchcraft and black magic.

memes.label: The Palace
memes.description: The royal seat where Princess Fiorimonde lives with her father the King, and where suitors arrive only to mysteriously vanish.

memes.label: The Palace Gardens
memes.description: The palace gardens where Princess Fiorimonde lured her suitors and where Arasmon overheard the plot to steal his harp, in "The Necklace of Princess Fiorimonde."

memes.label: "The Pedlar's Pack"
memes.description: The fifth story in Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, a fable about a dishonest pedlar and three animals who deceive each other in a chain of false promises.

memes.label: The Royal Castle
memes.description: The grand royal settings across Mary De Morgan's fairy tales — palaces of splendour and intrigue where kings rule, princesses dwell, and suitors meet mysterious fates.

memes.label: The Sacred Vow
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, the sacred vow is a binding promise that structures time — characters who keep their vows are rewarded, and those who break them are revealed as morally deficient."

memes.label: The Seashore
memes.description: A recurring liminal setting in multiple fairy tales, where characters encounter boundaries between the known world and the unknown beyond the sea.

memes.label: The Skull Goblet
memes.description: "The goblet formed from a human skull, filled with ruby wine, that the yellow hag offers Prince Michael at the climax of his trial — the final test of his perception and will."

memes.label: The Spellbound Village
memes.description: A gloomy, spellbound village on the moorland in "The Wanderings of Arasmon," cursed by a wizard and his dark elves until freed by Chrysea's song.

memes.label: "The Three Clever Kings"
memes.description: The seventh story in Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, about three cousins who each try to rule a kingdom and discover that wisdom lies not in power but in knowing one's own limitations.

memes.label: The Transformation of King Pierrot
memes.description: The pivotal event in which Princess Fiorimonde's first suitor, King Pierrot, is transformed into the first bead on the enchanted necklace.

memes.label: The Village of Discontent
memes.description: The unnamed village in "The Bread of Discontent" where a baker's fairy-tainted bread causes the entire community to descend into misery and quarrelling.

memes.label: "The Wanderings of Arasmon (Story)"
memes.description: The second story in Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, about a musician who loses his wife to an elves' curse and wanders the world seeking her.

memes.label: The Wedding Feast
memes.description: The recurring wedding and betrothal feasts across Mary De Morgan's collection, where marriage serves as both a desired union and a moment of magical peril.

memes.label: "The Wise Princess"
memes.description: The eighth and final story in Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, about a princess who masters all worldly knowledge only to discover that wisdom cannot teach happiness.

memes.label: The Witch's Drum
memes.description: "The terrible drum that the old witch beats to summon a grotesque assembly of elves, goblins, witches, and wicked fairies to her underground revel."

memes.label: The Witch's Hut
memes.description: The secluded dwelling of the old witch on a lonely mountain where Princess Fiorimonde learns sorcery and receives the enchanted necklace.

memes.label: The Witch's Sabbath
memes.description: The midnight supernatural gathering in the witch's underground chamber where dark elves, goblins, witches, and wicked fairies dance until cockcrow.

memes.label: The Wizard's Cave
memes.description: A deep underground cave where an ancient, wise wizard taught Princess Fernanda all his knowledge over three years in "The Wise Princess."

memes.label: The Wizard's Tower
memes.description: A mysterious round tower on a high hill, made of one single stone with no joins, where an ancient white wizard lived for hundreds of years in "The Heart of Princess Joan."

memes.label: themes-index
memes.description: Thematic index covering the major motifs, moral concepts, and narrative themes across Mary De Morgan's collection, dynamically generated from wiki entries.

memes.label: Transformation in De Morgan's Tales
memes.description: A cross-story analysis of physical transformation as De Morgan's central narrative device — how characters become objects, animals, or beggars, and what transformation reveals about moral identity.

memes.label: Truth
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, truth is a weapon, a shield, and a revealer — wielded by the wise to expose evil, denied by the foolish at their peril, and ultimately inescapable."

memes.label: Truth and Deception
memes.description: "Across De Morgan's 1880 collection, truth functions as a weapon wielded by the powerless — servants, pedlars, and honest suitors expose deceptions that beauty and power would conceal."

memes.label: "Uncopyright"
memes.description: "## Uncopyright > May you do good and not evil. May you find forgiveness for yourself and forgive others. May"

memes.label: Wealth
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, wealth is an unstable category — material riches are transient and vulnerable, while true wealth resides in love, music, and community."

memes.label: Wealth and Poverty
memes.description: "In De Morgan's 1880 tales, wealth is consistently portrayed as morally hazardous — those who have it risk losing their souls, while those who lack it may find truer forms of abundance."

memes.label: Wedding
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, the wedding serves as both a narrative destination and a site of moral testing — a public ritual where true love, false promises, and social judgment converge."

memes.label: Wedding as Ritual
memes.description: "In Mary De Morgan's 1880 collection, weddings serve as narrative pressure points — moments when fate pivots, identities shift, and the boundary between celebration and catastrophe is tested."

memes.label: Yolande
memes.description: Princess Fiorimonde's bright-faced maid who suspects her mistress's wickedness and helps break the enchanted necklace's spell.
